steps.title: Spot the cause-and-effect relationship
steps.content:
The first sentence states that Arabic-speaking Muslim states ruled Al-Andalus for centuries.

The second sentence describes how Arabic words entered the Spanish language.

This is a consequence of that prolonged Arabic-language dominance—the linguistic outcome naturally follows from the historical condition.

steps.title: Match the causal signal
steps.content:
Consequently directly marks a cause-and-effect relationship, showing how centuries of Arabic rule naturally produced linguistic influence in Spanish.

The other three fail to establish this logic: instead creates a false contrast, while specifically and for example would require the first sentence to introduce multiple effects or categories to specify or exemplify—which it doesn't.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
